live_path.role: CLI entrypoint parses --owner and forwards it to the service layer.
Coordinates owner resolution, base-record creation, routing-key derivation, and payload emission.
Derives the effective owner and owner_source as explicit or default.
Builds the stored base record with only name, status, and owner.
Computes routing_key from the already-resolved owner and name.
Copies the stored record and appends owner_source and routing_key to the emitted payload.

owner_source.logic: Returns explicit when a non-blank --owner value is present; otherwise returns default using config/defaults.json.

routing_key.logic: Computed from effective_owner and name after owner resolution, using slugified owner:name format.

test_observations.observation: sync_item(..., owner='pm-oncall') emits owner='pm-oncall', owner_source='explicit', routing_key='pm-oncall:launch-checklist'.
sync_item(...) without owner emits the default owner from config/defaults.json, owner_source='default', and a routing_key built from that default owner.
CLI parsing of --owner reaches the same emitted payload shape as the direct service call.
Docs are expected to mention --owner, owner_source, routing_key, sync_item, and the configured default owner.

rejected_decoys.reason: Unused legacy helper; the live path imports and calls make_record instead.
Unused legacy helper; the live path imports build_routing_key from sync_app/serializer.py.
Reads owner_source from an existing record, but no live caller uses it in the request path.
Design-intent note only; it explicitly says it does not describe the live implementation.
Historical rolled-back draft that states the stale storage-layer theory rather than the live path.
Escalation hypothesis contradicted by the traced call chain and tests.
